meta.title: About — The Studio
meta.description: ATLVS Technologies builds the platform for production. ATLVS, GVTEWAY, COMPVSS — three apps, one schema. Shipped by operators who've run the room.
meta.ogImageTitle: Built by the People Who Run the Show.

hero.title: Built by the People Who Run the Show.
hero.lead: ATLVS Technologies builds the platform for production. Every module came out of opening a spreadsheet for the third time and saying "why isn't this in the platform."
hero.subLead: Three apps. One schema. Forty-seven modules. We ship to real productions the weekend before each release. The work decides what stays in.

threeApps.title: Three apps. One schema.
threeApps.body: ATLVS, GVTEWAY, COMPVSS share identity, data, and design. One database underneath.

rls.title: RLS at the database.
rls.body: Tenant walled in Postgres, not in the UI. Immutable audit log on every change. Signed webhooks. Standard on every tier.

tested.title: Tested in the room.
tested.body: We ship to a real production the weekend before each release. If it doesn't survive load-in, it doesn't ship.

perOrg.title: Per org. Never per seat.
perOrg.body: No per-seat trap. No per-scan tax. No velvet-rope gates on things that should be standard. Pricing reads like one number, not a tax form.

procoreParity.title: Procore parity
procoreParity.body: RFIs, submittals, daily logs, punch list, payment apps, change orders. Plus everything Procore won't touch.

threeApps.title: Three apps on one schema
threeApps.body: ATLVS, GVTEWAY, COMPVSS shipped on one database — workspace, portal, field.

kbyg.title: KBYG event guides
kbyg.body: One Know-Before-You-Go, authored once, rendered per-persona across portal and field.

proposals.title: Proposals, signed in place
proposals.body: Twenty-three block types. Live pricing. E-sign in the page. One URL replaces the PDF email thread.

stripeConnect.title: Stripe Connect payouts + advancing
stripeConnect.body: Vendor payouts via Connect. Sixteen typed deliverables with history, comments, attachments.

firstProduction.title: First production shipped
firstProduction.body: Staffed and scanned a 2,000-cap show end-to-end on the platform. Stage went down. The platform didn't.

studio.heading: Who Builds It.
studio.body: ATLVS is a production + technology studio. We've staffed residencies, touring runs, private launches, gallery programs, brand activations. We shipped software for every one of them. The platform is a decade of in-the-field instinct, compressed into one workspace.

meta.title: AI For Production — Drafts From Your Workspace, Never The Public Internet
meta.description: An assistant that drafts riders, incident reports, call sheets, proposals, recaps, and safety briefs from your live workspace data. Anthropic Claude under the hood; per-org scope; never trained on your data.

jsonLd.description: Production-AI assistant grounded in your workspace, powered by Anthropic Claude.

hero.subtitle: An assistant that reads your projects, talent, vendors, schedule, and budgets — then drafts the things production teams write every day. Riders, incident reports, call sheets, proposals, recaps, safety briefs. Anthropic Claude under the hood, scoped to your tenant, never trained on your data.

drafts.title: What It Drafts.
drafts.subtitle: Six production workflows where the assistant takes a draft 80% of the way and you ship the last 20%.

rules.title: What The AI Does — And Won't Do.
rules.subtitle: Production teams asked us where the lines are. Here's the canon.

items.0: Reads only your org's data. RLS at the database — not the app.
items.1: Drafts; does not auto-publish. PM, EHS, or TM holds the publish key.
items.2: Flags inferences vs. grounded facts. You see what's invented.
items.3: Conversations log in your tenant, queryable, exportable, not shared.
items.4: Anthropic Claude — never trained on your records.
items.5: Per-org cost cap. Runaway usage is impossible.

otherOrgs.q: Does the AI see other organizations' data?
otherOrgs.a: No. Every tool the assistant can call is scoped to your organization. RLS at the database — your tenant is walled off in Postgres, not in the app layer. The AI cannot reach into anyone else's data, by design.

training.q: Do you train on our data?
training.a: No. We don't train models. We're a production platform that uses Anthropic Claude under the hood — your conversations stay in your tenant, your records stay yours.

model.a: Claude Sonnet 4.6 by default for day-to-day work, Claude Opus 4.7 available per conversation when you need deeper reasoning. Switch in the conversation menu; cost is metered per-org with configurable monthly caps.

wrong.q: What if the AI gets something wrong?
wrong.a: Every output is a draft. The PM, EHS lead, or tour manager publishes. Auto-publish workflows are configurable but opt-in. The assistant also flags when it's inferring vs. when it's working from explicit data.

freeTier.a: Yes. The Free tier includes light AI assistance. Crew and Production tiers include the streaming assistant with conversation history. Festival tier adds custom system prompts and dedicated model selection.

cta.title: ATLVS Is Open.
cta.subtitle: Free for small teams. AI included on Crew and up.

metadata.title: Careers — Build for Production
metadata.description: We're a small studio shipping the operating system for live events. Open roles ship work that surfaces on real shows — measured in load-ins survived, not story points burned.

hero.title: Build for Production.
hero.body: We're building the platform the live-events industry has needed for fifteen years — one operating system that runs the producer's desk, the crew's phone, and the client's portal off the same database. Small studio, deep ownership, real shows.

crew.title: Crew, not staff
crew.body: Small group, deep ownership. Every role touches every shell — marketing, ATLVS, portal, mobile.

tested.title: Tested on the water
tested.body: We ship to real shows the weekend before each release. Bias to load-in survival over feature theater.

pricing.title: Pricing is an ethic
pricing.body: Per org, not per seat. We hold that line internally — no token economies, no perf review caste system.

brandFirst.title: Brand-first engineering
brandFirst.body: The platform reads like a magazine. Brutal where it should be, breathing where it can. Code follows.

distributed.title: Distributed by default
distributed.body: Studio in Miami, crew across timezones. Async-first. Travel for site visits and shows, not standups.

equity.title: Equity that means something
equity.body: Real ownership stake, four-year vest, ten-year exercise window. We pay competitively in cash too.

empty.body: We hire deliberately and post openings here when they exist. If your background is unusual or specifically aligned with what we're building, we read every thoughtful intro that comes through the contact form.

hiring.q: Is the studio hiring right now?
hiring.a: We're a small team and we hire deliberately. When we do open a role, it lands here first. If nothing's posted, it's truly closed — but we always read thoughtful intros at the contact link below.

visas.a: For senior engineering and design roles, yes — case by case. We'll be explicit on each posting whether sponsorship is on the table.

remote.a: Distributed by default. The Miami studio is open if you're nearby and want desk time, but no role requires you to be there.

interview.a: Three calls: intro with a founder, working session with a peer (no whiteboard puzzles — we look at real work), and a paid trial week if both sides want to keep going.

notHire.q: What do you NOT hire for?
notHire.a: Project managers, scrum coaches, and "head of" titles where the work is meetings. Engineering managers code; design leads design; founders sell.

cta.title: Not Listed? Tell Us Anyway.
cta.subtitle: If your background is unusual or specifically built for what we ship, write us. The studio reads every note.

fields.name: Name

start.q: Do I need to call to get started?
start.a: No. /signup and you're on Free — forever. 14-day trial on Crew or Production, no card. The studio is for Festival access, custom integrations, or a walkthrough.

responseTime.q: How fast does the studio respond?
responseTime.a: Studio: one business day. Concierge on Crew: 24 business hours. Production: 4. Festival: contractual SLA, usually one hour for P0.

producer.q: Can we talk to a producer, not a salesperson?
producer.a: We'll schedule a call with someone from the studio who's run productions on the platform. Check the box in the form.

location.a: Distributed across the US — producers in NYC, LA, Miami, Chicago, Austin. Happy to show up in-person wherever the work is.

metadata.title: Help — Crew Support
metadata.description: Self-serve guides, status, and direct support channels. Concierge for customers — triaged by tier. Festival gets an SLA.

hero.title: Crew Support.
hero.subtitle: Self-serve guides for the patient, concierge for the urgent, hotline for the load-in. Choose your channel by how live the show is.

fieldGuide.title: Field Guide
fieldGuide.body: Long-form guides on every shell — how to run a load-in week, set up advancing, wire Stripe payouts, configure portal access.

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.body: What shipped this week. Every release, with context on why it matters at load-in.

concierge.title: Concierge
concierge.body: For customers — triaged by tier (Free, Crew, Production, Festival). Festival gets a four-hour SLA.

hotline.title: Production hotline
hotline.body: Live show in trouble? The hotline is staffed by producers, not bots. Available 24h during your event window if you've notified us.

signIn.title: Can't sign in
signIn.body: Try the magic-link flow first — it bypasses password issues. If your email isn't recognized, an admin needs to invite you. The producer or workspace owner can issue an invite from Settings → People.

webhook.title: Webhook isn't firing
webhook.body: Check the audit log first (Settings → Audit). Webhooks ship signed; if signature verification fails on your end, the receipt won't register. Re-check the secret + the SHA-256 verification snippet in the docs.

mobileOffline.title: Mobile app stuck offline
mobileOffline.body: Pull-to-refresh on the call sheet to force a sync. If a queued action shows as pending for more than 30 seconds, sign out and back in — that re-pairs the offline cache to the active workspace.

reportBug.q: Where do I report a bug?
reportBug.a: Email support@atlvs.pro with the URL where it happened and a screenshot if you have one. We tag every report with the audit log entry so we can see exactly what happened on our side.

statusPage.q: Do you have a status page?
statusPage.a: Yes — status.atlvs.pro. Production incidents post within ten minutes. Maintenance windows announce 48 hours ahead.

phoneSupport.a: The production hotline is the closest thing — staffed by producers during active event windows. We don't run general phone support; concierge email gets to a human within hours, faster on Festival.

invoice.q: Where do I find my invoice or change billing details?
invoice.a: Inside ATLVS: Settings → Billing. Stripe-hosted self-serve, so you can update card, download past invoices, or change billing email there directly.

cancel.a: Settings → Billing → Cancel subscription. Your data stays available read-only for 90 days; export anything you need from Settings → Exports during that window.

lostAccess.q: I lost access to my admin account — what now?
lostAccess.a: Contact concierge from the email on file with the org. We require a chain of identity proof to transfer ownership — typically founder/officer documentation. Plan ~3 business days for the handover.

cta.title: Still Stuck? Talk to a Producer.
cta.subtitle: Concierge email is the fastest path. We respond within hours, faster on Festival.

metadata.title: ATLVS — Production Management Software for Live Experiences
metadata.description: ATLVS Technologies — production management, crew operations, and ticketing software built for live events. Three apps, one schema: ATLVS for producers, COMPVSS for crew, GVTEWAY for guests. Pitch through wrap on a single platform.

hero.subtitleGvteway: runs the portal. Pitch through wrap on a single connected platform for live experiential production.
hero.disclaimer: Free forever for small teams · No credit card required · Per-org pricing

threeApps.title: Three apps, one schema.
threeApps.body: ATLVS Technologies ships three connected products on a shared Postgres database. Adopt one or run the full platform — every record flows across producer console, field PWA, and stakeholder portal.

atlvs.body: Projects, RFIs, submittals, daily logs, advancing, finance, procurement, AI assistant. The producer's console — pitch through wrap.

compvss.body: Scheduling, certifications, gate scan, shift clock-in, incidents, medical. Offline-first PWA. Sub-100ms scans on one-bar LTE.

lifecycle.body: Every production runs through the same eight phases. The schema follows the phases, the audit log follows the schema, and every approval gate blocks the next phase until the previous one closes.

difference.title: Not a CRM stitched to a PM tool.
difference.body: Most production-operations stacks bolt together a CRM, a project tool, a doc tool, and a ticketing integration. ATLVS Technologies owns the entire stack — every app built in-house, every record connected.

endToEnd.body: Pitch through wrap, all eight phases, in one workspace. No integration tax, no schema reconciliation.

perOrgNotPerSeat.title: Per Org, Not Per Seat

rrr312.title: Black Coffee at the Race Track

rrr023.title: Heineken Turn 4 Nightclub
rrr023.sub: Motorsports · F1 Las Vegas Grand Prix

whatIsAtlvs.a: ATLVS Technologies is the platform behind live experiential production. Three connected apps on one database: ATLVS for producers (projects, RFIs, advancing, finance, procurement, AI), COMPVSS for crew (scheduling, certifications, gate scan, incidents), and GVTEWAY for guests and stakeholders (ticketing, proposals, twelve personas). Same record across all three, pitch through wrap.

whoIsItFor.q: Who is the ATLVS platform for?
whoIsItFor.a: Production teams running real live work. Festivals, residencies, touring, fabrication shops, brand activations, broadcast compounds, private events. The schema is generic; the vernacular is specific. If your org has a load-in, a manifest, vendors, and a wrap — it fits.

pricing.a: Per organization, not per seat. Free forever for solo operators and small teams. Crew opens up the team. Production unlocks every module plus multi-project. Festival is multi-org with SSO, custom DPA, and a 99.9% SLA. Full grid at /pricing.

vendorPayouts.a: Stripe Connect. Vendors onboard a payout account through their portal. You approve the PO. Payouts route directly. Money never crosses our books. ACH, card, international wire. Signed webhooks for every event.

compvssOffline.a: Yes. COMPVSS is an offline-first PWA. Gate scan, shift clock-in, daily log, incident, medic intake — all queue locally and sync on signal return. Sub-100ms scan even on one-bar LTE. Tested at 15,000-guest gates.

whoBuilds.a: ATLVS Technologies is a GHXSTSHIP Industries company — the technology arm of an experiential production house headquartered in Miami with offices in New York, Chicago, and Los Angeles. ATLVS, COMPVSS, and GVTEWAY are built in-house by people who have shipped real shows.

dataSecurity.a: Row-level security at the database — your tenant is walled off in Postgres, not in the app layer. Every change writes to an immutable audit log (actor, IP, user agent, before/after). Signed webhooks (HMAC-SHA256). File shares self-expire. SOC-2 attestation plus custom DPA on Festival tier.

aiAssistant.q: Is the AI assistant useful or marketing?
aiAssistant.a: Useful. It reads your workspace data — never the public internet — and drafts riders, RFPs, call sheets, recaps, safety briefs, and incident reports. Streaming, conversation-persisted in your tenant. Anthropic Claude under the hood.

exportData.a: Always. CSV exports on every tier from every list view. Full data export via Settings → Exports on Production and above. You own the data; we host it. Cancel and you get a 90-day read-only window to pull everything.

fieldNotes.title: How the 8-Phase Lifecycle keeps a build on course

comparison.title: Every Module, Every Tier.
comparison.intro: The full grid. If a module you need is gated above where you are,

perSeat.a: Per org. Production is unlimited users for $199/month. Adding the crew never becomes a surprise line item.

freeReallyFree.a: Forever. Basic projects, guest + artist portals, three users, up to 100 portal credentials per project. No card.

trialWorks.a: Crew and Production unlock every feature in the tier for 14 days. No card up front. Don't upgrade by day 14? You drop to Free — your data stays where it is.

changeTiers.a: Anytime. Upgrades hit instantly. Downgrades kick in at the end of the billing period. Nothing gets deleted — features above your tier go read-only until you come back.

nonprofits.a: 30% off Crew and Production for registered nonprofits and community arts programs. Email hello@atlvs.pro with your 501(c)(3) or equivalent.

perScanFee.a: No. Scan all weekend. Every weekend.

festivalVsProduction.q: What's in Festival that isn't in Production?
festivalVsProduction.a: Multi-org with SSO. Custom roles + access policies. SOC-2 attestation pack. Custom DPA. Dedicated CSM. 99.9% uptime SLA. Source-available license. Custom integrations we build with you.

selfHost.a: Source-available license ships with Festival. Managed infrastructure is the recommended route — handles security, storage, and uptime.

dataAfterCancel.q: What happens to my data if I cancel?
dataAfterCancel.a: Export everything — CSV or JSON — anytime. After cancel, your data lives 90 days read-only (re-subscribe and you're back), then purges. Signed logs of the purge on request. You own the data.

q1.question: O que a plataforma faz?
q1.answer: Quarenta e sete módulos em três apps que compartilham um banco de dados. ATLVS é o workspace de operações — RFIs, submittals, daily logs, punch, advancing, financeiro, procurement, IA. GVTEWAY é o portal de stakeholders. COMPVSS é o PWA offline-first do campo — scan de portão, ponto eletrônico, incidentes, médico, briefing diário de segurança.

q2.question: Pra quem é?
q2.answer: Times de produção que rodam trabalho real ao vivo. Festivais, residências, turnês, oficinas de fabricação, ativações de marca, compounds de broadcast, eventos privados. O schema é genérico; o vocabulário é específico.

q3.question: Como funciona o preço?
q3.answer: Por organização, não por assento. Grátis para sempre para times pequenos. Crew abre o time. Production libera cada módulo. Festival é multi-org com SSO, DPA personalizado e SLA de 99.9%.

q4.question: O app de campo funciona offline mesmo?
q4.answer: Sim. COMPVSS é um PWA offline-first. Scan, ponto, daily log, intake de incidente, intake médico — tudo fica em fila localmente e sincroniza quando o sinal volta. Testado em portões com 15,000 convidados.

work.title: Work

sales.title: Sales

procurement.title: Procurement

production.title: Production

people.title: People

construction.title: Construction

safety.title: Safety

ai.title: AI assistant

architecture.title: Same Rails. Every Module.
architecture.body1: List, detail, create, edit. Every module rides the same rails — with status flow where it counts (invoices, proposals, POs, tasks, RFIs). Validation at the door. Tenant walled at the database.
architecture.body2: Same rails mean the team learns ATLVS once. New modules ship in days. The work changes — the workflow holds.

bullets.rls: RLS at the database. Tenant walled in Postgres.
bullets.roleAware: Role-aware on every action. Enforced server-side, not in the UI.
bullets.auditLog: Immutable audit log. Actor, IP, before, after. Forever.
bullets.aiGrounded: AI grounded in your workspace. Never the public internet.
bullets.stripeConnect: Stripe Connect vendor payouts. Money never crosses our books.
bullets.signedWebhooks: Signed webhooks (HMAC-SHA256) on every state change.
bullets.selfExpiring: Self-expiring file shares. No public buckets.

owner.body: Full access. Billing, org, members, integrations, branding. Runs the business while the show runs.

controller.body: Finance + procurement. Invoices, expenses, budgets, POs, payouts, audit log.

projectManager.body: Daily driver. Projects, tasks, schedule, RFIs, submittals, advancing, crew, proposals, clients.

what.a: The production operations workspace. Red-branded, desk-bound, always on. Forty-seven modules — proposals, RFIs, submittals, daily logs, punch, inspections, advancing, finance, procurement, AI. Where the production lives.

roleAware.a: Ten roles (owner, admin, controller, collaborator, contractor, crew, client, viewer, and cameos). Every capability is gated by role — enforced at the database via RLS, not in the UI.

brand.a: Self-serve brand skins under Settings → Branding land on Festival. Production gets logo + accent. Every tier respects your org's accent color in the chrome.

ai.a: Anthropic Claude under the hood. A fast model for daily drafting on Production. Deep-reasoning model on Festival. Every thread logs to your workspace, scoped tight.

meta.title: COMPVSS — The Field
meta.description: Offline-first PWA. Sub-100ms gate scan. Shift clock-in, daily log, incident, medic, driver, guard, warehouse. Installs from the browser in one tap.

schema.name: COMPVSS — The Field
schema.description: Offline-first PWA for production crew. Gate scan, clock-in, incident, medic, driver, guard, warehouse.

hero.body: Installs from the browser in one tap. Gate scan, shift clock-in, medic triage, incident, driver run, guard tour, warehouse, daily safety brief. Works on one-bar LTE.

modules.heading: What the Crew Runs in the Field.

today.title: Today
today.body: Role-routed landing — call sheet, pinned tasks, tonight's shift.

gateScan.title: Gate + ticket scan
gateScan.body: QR + barcode. Accreditation zone logic. Sub-100ms. Offline or on.

doorPass.title: Door pass
doorPass.body: Same KBYG as the portal — scoped crew-side to radio, PPE, SOPs.

shift.title: Shift
shift.body: Geo-verified clock-in / break / meal-credit / clock-out. Manual fallback included.

warehouse.title: Warehouse
warehouse.body: Pick / put-away scans. Offline queue replays in order. Nothing goes missing.

incident.title: Incident · medic · safeguarding
incident.body: Photos, location, witnesses. Admin + medic + safeguarding lead paged instantly. Encrypted at rest.

offlineFirst.heading: The Cell Tower Is Not Your Problem.
offlineFirst.body1: Gate scan, manual lookup, tonight's call sheet — cached on device. Scans queue locally and replay in order the second signal returns. Nothing drops. Nobody waits.
offlineFirst.body2: Install from the browser — open the link, tap Install, full-screen launcher on the home screen. Done before soundcheck.

bullets.install: Installs from the browser — one tap
bullets.queue: Offline scan queue replays in order. Zero drops.
bullets.scanStates: Clear scan states — accepted · duplicate · voided · not found
bullets.permissions: Camera + GPS requested only when needed
bullets.tabBar: Bottom tab bar — five field-side actions
bullets.contrast: High-contrast every screen. Readable across the gate.

native.q: Is COMPVSS a native iOS / Android app?
native.a: No. PWA. Open the URL, tap Install, full-screen launcher. No App Store review. No fleet update the morning of. No version drift across your 400-person gate team.

noSignal.q: What if the venue has no cell signal?
noSignal.a: The scanner, tonight's call sheet, and the door pass cache on device. Scans queue locally and replay in order the moment signal returns.

gateSpeed.a: Sub-100ms server-side. US cell adds 200–400ms round-trip. Offline scans are instant locally. Duplicates, voided, not-found — distinct states, all on-screen in a glance.

offlineCheckin.a: Really. Scans queue on device. Replay in order when signal returns. During an offline window, duplicates the scanner can't yet confirm are flagged pending — the server reconciles on sync.

camera.a: For QR + barcode scans. Manual input is the fallback for damaged codes or blocked permissions. The crew member at the gate never waits on IT.

cta.title: Open the Field App.
cta.subtitle: Installs on any phone. No App Store. No fleet update. Ready in a tap.

meta.title: GVTEWAY — The Portal
meta.description: Twelve personas. Each their lane. Artists see riders. Vendors see POs. Clients see proposals. Same project, RLS at the database.

jsonLd.name: GVTEWAY — The Portal
jsonLd.description: External stakeholder portal. Twelve personas, each scoped to their lane via RLS at the database.

hero.title: Twelve Personas. Each Their Lane.
hero.body: Artists advance. Vendors quote. Clients sign. Guests scan in. Crew clocks on. Same project. Twelve scoped reads. RLS at the database.

personas.title: Twelve Personas. Same Database.
personas.body: Every persona arrives at its own lane — nav, flows, deliverables tuned to the role. The artist never sees the sponsor's invoice. The delegation never sees the vendor's COI. Enforced in Postgres.

artist.title: Artist
artist.body: Tech rider, hospitality rider, input list, stage plot, guest list, schedule, travel.

vendor.title: Vendor
vendor.body: Submissions, pull list, POs, invoices, COI + W-9 uploads, training records.

client.title: Client
client.body: Proposals signed in place, deliverables, invoices via card/ACH, messages, files.

sponsor.title: Sponsor
sponsor.body: Entitlements tracker, activations, brand assets, reporting, impressions.

guest.title: Guest
guest.body: Tickets — buy, claim, transfer. Schedule. Day-of logistics. Rideshare hand-off.

crew.title: Crew
crew.body: Call sheet, time submission, advances, safety briefing, SOPs.

delegation.title: Delegation
delegation.body: Entries, rate card, training venues, meetings, cases, transport, accommodation, visa.

media.title: Media
media.body: MPC rate card, accommodation, shuttles, press-conf RSVP, info-on-demand.

vip.title: VIP + Hospitality
vip.body: Dedicated fleet, hotel allocation, itinerary, guest list, package access.

volunteer.title: Volunteer
volunteer.body: Application, training, shift schedule, uniform pickup, door pass.

athlete.title: Athlete
athlete.body: Training bookings, resident services, safeguarding, visa, privacy self-service.

privacy.title: Privacy (all)
privacy.body: Every persona gets DSAR self-service + consent management. GDPR, handled.

proposals.title: Proposals That Close Themselves.
proposals.body: Twenty-three block types. Scroll-spy nav. Per-phase contract refs. Add-on pickers with live totals. E-sign in place — typed or drawn. Revocable share links. Version history. Engagement analytics on every scroll. Clean PDF if legal still wants paper.

kbyg.title: Written Once. Read Twelve Ways.
kbyg.body: One Know-Before-You-Go doc in ATLVS. Renders per-persona in the portal and in the field. Artists get riders + catering. Crew sees call sheet + radio + PPE. Guests see logistics + tickets. Seventeen section types. One source, twelve reads.

security.title: RLS at the Database.
security.body: Every record is scoped to its org and project in Postgres. Share links are signed, expirable, revocable, audit-logged. Nobody slips from the sponsor lounge into the artist compound — access enforced on the data, not in the UI.

items.perProjectPortals: Per-project portals — one link, one project
items.signedLinks: Signed share links with expiry + revocation
items.everyViewLogged: Every view logged in engagement analytics
items.eSign: E-sign in place — typed or drawn, IP + timestamp captured

q1.a: The portal. Blue-branded. Every guest, artist, vendor, sponsor, and collaborator arrives through their own lane. Twelve personas — each sees only its scope.

q2.a: Read-only? Share the link — they're in. Contributing (riders, COIs, signatures)? A lightweight signup creates a credential scoped to that project. Fast by design.

q3.a: Festival. Your mark, colors, email, custom domain. Clients see your brand. Vendors see your vendors' brand.

q4.q: How is data isolated between projects?
q4.a: Postgres RLS. Every record is scoped to its org and project. The link you share maps to one project — nobody slips from one to another.

q5.q: Can one credential access two projects?
q5.a: Yes. A vendor on one project and a sponsor on another switches cleanly from the portal home. One login, two scoped reads.

cta.title: Twelve Personas. One Source.
cta.subtitle: Wired default. Reskin if you want. Use it if you don't.

metadata.description: ATLVS — production operations workspace. GVTEWAY — stakeholder portal. COMPVSS — offline-first field PWA. Same record from the office, the stakeholder, and the gate.

hero.title: Three Apps. One Schema.
hero.body: ATLVS, GVTEWAY, COMPVSS. One database underneath. Same record, three optimized surfaces — your office, your stakeholders, your crew, all reading the same truth.

atlvs.body: Forty-seven modules in one sidebar. Pitch through wrap. Proposals to payouts, RFIs to inspections, advancing to AI.

bullets.3: Role-aware on every action, enforced server-side

gvteway.body: Artist, vendor, client, sponsor, guest, crew, delegation, media, VIP, hospitality, volunteer, athlete. Same project, scoped reads.

bullets.1: Proposals signed in place — 23 block types

compvss.title: Offline. Sub-100ms.
compvss.body: Gate scan, shift clock-in, daily log, incident, medic, driver, guard, warehouse. Installs from the browser in one tap.

bullets.3: Tonight's call sheet + KBYG in pocket

industries.heading: Live Work, Whatever the Shape
industries.body: Each shape gets a tuned setup — starter templates, default role matrix, vernacular notes from operators who've done it.

0.q: How are the three apps different?
0.a: ATLVS is the production operations workspace — where the production lives. GVTEWAY is the stakeholder portal — twelve personas, each their lane. COMPVSS is the offline-first field PWA. One database underneath.

1.a: Every tier ships all three. Most teams open with ATLVS + one GVTEWAY persona (artist or client), add COMPVSS at load-in, then expand. Your tempo.

2.q: Can other orgs see my data?
2.a: No. RLS at the database — walled in Postgres, not in the app layer. A credential in one org cannot read or touch anything in another. Enforced in the data.

3.a: Festival. Your mark, your colors, your email, your custom domain on every persona. The brand is yours.
